name: UDP Transport Protocol
description: Quiz focused exclusively on the UDP protocol, its characteristics, advantages and demultiplexing.

questions.question: What is the essential nature of the transport service provided by the UDP protocol?
options: It requires prior connection setup through the use of handshaking.
It is a best-effort service where packets may be lost.
It guarantees the perfectly sequential and ordered delivery of packets.
It operates at the network layer to find the fastest physical route.

questions.question: One of UDP's main mechanical characteristics is that it:
options: Has rigorous flow control to avoid overloading the target nodes
Applies slow-start algorithms, testing the real capacity of the network
Lacks congestion control and sends data very fast
Limits the send rate whenever warnings are issued by the routers

questions.question: Due to its characteristics, which of these scenarios is the most suitable for the use of the UDP protocol?
options: Banking systems where absolutely no loss can be tolerated.
Video streaming applications that tolerate losses and demand speed.
Legacy web systems based purely on the old HTTP 1 architecture.
Email access and sending processed under the SMTP protocol.

questions.question: Regarding connection complexity, what perfectly characterizes the UDP sender and receiver?
options: UDP has a very small header and keeps no state
UDP keeps complex records of open and active sessions
UDP requires the client to store the session's receive window
UDP uses sophisticated timers to define the timeouts

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the 'Checksum' field present in the UDP segment header?
options: To strongly encrypt the entire payload to ensure confidentiality.
To detect the existence of flipped bits or errors in the received packet.
To prevent router congestion by delaying the output of new packets.
To digitally sign the packet, natively blocking IP spoofing tactics.

questions.question: How does the connectionless demultiplexing process (UDP) direct the received segment to the correct socket?
options: The host examines only the destination port contained in the received segment.
The receiver processes a four-tuple of information, crossing IPs and their ports.
The system requests that the sender report its physical route before transmission.
The core router reads the information recorded in the client's application layer.

questions.question: If an application needs highly reliable transfer but decides to run over UDP (such as HTTP/3 with QUIC), what must be done?
options: The developer will need to swap the network cards, enabling the optical fibers
The network layer itself will be forced to recreate the lost datagram on the path.
This extra reliability will need to be built at the application layer.
The edge routers will implement flow control by holding back the packets.

questions.question: Why is UDP considered capable of starting to send data faster than the TCP protocol?
options: It halves the propagation delay time on the optical-fiber links.
It avoids buildup in the routers' output queues by compressing each datagram.
It eliminates the initial delay since it does not require a handshake to create the connection.
It transmits the packets at the speed of light, eliminating the processing time.

questions.question: The UDP Checksum is considered a relatively weak protection. For what structural mathematical reason does this happen?
options: It fails and does not change if a compensating inversion of certain bits occurs.
It deletes the secret packet whenever a high queuing delay is identified.
It abruptly ends the transmission when it discovers routing failures on the channel.
It requires enormous CPU computing power, making its verification slow.

questions.question: When creating a UDP socket in network programming (using DatagramSocket), what does the system mandatorily require from the socket's creator?
options: The dedicated reservation of all the bandwidth that the packet network can provide.
The definition of the alternative transport protocol focused on the session layer.
The immediate and explicit specification of the local port number of that host.
The performance of a three-way cryptographic handshake to shield the system.
